generatedSentence: The seasoned plumber, a master of tricky fixes, meticulously reviewed his logbook in his cluttered chamber. His old credentials for advanced enrollment were long expired, yet his practical knowledge was vast. He sipped his coffee, a boost of caffeine fueling his organized mind. His young assistant watched, learning the nuances of his behavior, a blend of gruffness and underlying decency and tolerance. There was always a queue at his entrance. News from the grand palace spoke of an ancient prophecy, a dire fable suggesting a force would destroy the city’s water fort. A sudden flurry of thick flakes threatened the delivery of critical supplies by truck, complicating efforts. A main pipeline was now precariously dangling, creating an immediate overload for the city. The lookout reported the situation was awfully critical. Clad in his specialized jumpsuit, the plumber surveyed the oceanfront damage, his eyebrow furrowed. A section of pipe now jiggles, causing a dangerous overlap. He donned his earphones, tuning out the panicked jury of officials. His only option was to cobble together a solution, a complex biology of pipes, making a beacon of hope. This duality of chaos and creation was his symbol, his touch as silky as saffron. The crisis began to abate, the finale a success. A luxuriant flow of blue water, sweet as a lollipop, marked his signature repair. He had achieved the impossible. Under the temporary awning, the previous jumble of pipes now sang with a steady phonetic hum. The satisfaction tickled him, seeing only the memory of jaundice in the rust. He imagined his beehive in the distant paddock, no need for an autopsy.
panelSentences: The seasoned plumber, a master of tricky fixes, meticulously reviewed his logbook in his cluttered chamber. His old credentials for advanced enrollment were long expired, yet his practical knowledge was vast. He sipped his coffee, a boost of caffeine fueling his organized mind. His young assistant watched, learning the nuances of his behavior, a blend of gruffness and underlying decency and tolerance. There was always a queue at his entrance.

News from the grand palace spoke of an ancient prophecy, a dire fable suggesting a force would destroy the city’s water fort. A sudden flurry of thick flakes threatened the delivery of critical supplies by truck, complicating efforts. A main pipeline was now precariously dangling, creating an immediate overload for the city. The lookout reported the situation was awfully critical.

Clad in his specialized jumpsuit, the plumber surveyed the oceanfront damage, his eyebrow furrowed. A section of pipe now jiggles, causing a dangerous overlap. He donned his earphones, tuning out the panicked jury of officials. His only option was to cobble together a solution, a complex biology of pipes, making a beacon of hope. This duality of chaos and creation was his symbol, his touch as silky as saffron.

The crisis began to abate, the finale a success. A luxuriant flow of blue water, sweet as a lollipop, marked his signature repair. He had achieved the impossible. Under the temporary awning, the previous jumble of pipes now sang with a steady phonetic hum. The satisfaction tickled him, seeing only the memory of jaundice in the rust. He imagined his beehive in the distant paddock, no need for an autopsy.
rawPanelSentences: The seasoned **plumber**, a master of **tricky** fixes, meticulously reviewed his **logbook** in his cluttered **chamber**. His old **credentials** for advanced **enrollment** were long **expired**, yet his practical knowledge was vast. He sipped his **coffee**, a boost of **caffeine** fueling his **organized** mind. His young **assistant** watched, learning the nuances of his **behavior**, a blend of gruffness and underlying **decency** and **tolerance**. There was always a queue at his **entrance**.

News from the grand **palace** spoke of an ancient **prophecy**, a dire **fable** suggesting a force would **destroy** the city’s water **fort**. A sudden **flurry** of **thick** **flakes** threatened the **delivery** of critical **supplies** by **truck**, complicating efforts. A main pipeline was now precariously **dangling**, creating an immediate **overload** for the city. The **lookout** reported the situation was **awfully** critical.

Clad in his specialized **jumpsuit**, the plumber surveyed the **oceanfront** damage, his **eyebrow** furrowed. A section of pipe now **jiggles**, causing a dangerous **overlap**. He donned his **earphones**, tuning out the panicked **jury** of officials. His only **option** was to **cobble** together a solution, a complex **biology** of pipes, making a **beacon** of hope. This **duality** of chaos and creation was his **symbol**, his touch as **silky** as **saffron**.

The crisis began to **abate**, the **finale** a success. A **luxuriant** flow of **blue** water, sweet as a **lollipop**, marked his **signature** repair. He had **achieved** the impossible. Under the temporary **awning**, the previous **jumble** of pipes now sang with a steady **phonetic** hum. The satisfaction **tickled** him, seeing only the memory of **jaundice** in the rust. He imagined his **beehive** in the distant **paddock**, no need for an **autopsy**.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, dynamic American comic book style, thick white gutters, clean black borders. Our protagonist, a grizzled plumber, wearing a worn, grease-stained denim jumpsuit, a large antique brass wrench gleaming at his hip, and a cybernetic patch over his left eye. TOP-LEFT PANEL: The grizzled plumber sifting through old credentials in his cluttered, caffeine-fueled workshop, low-angle shot. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: A wide shot of a snow-covered grand palace with a massive, twisted pipeline precariously dangling, snow flakes falling, high-angle shot. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: The plumber in his jumpsuit, wearing earphones, expertly cobbling pipes together at a stormy oceanfront, focused close-up on his hands. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: A tranquil scene of clear blue water flowing smoothly through repaired pipes under a temporary awning, the plumber's back to the viewer, admiring his work, wide shot. cel-shading, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting. NO text, NO speech bubbles, NO dialogue.

definitions.definition: Hard to deal with, complicated
definitions.example: They were in a tricky situation.

definitions.definition: Adept at using deception
definitions.example: A tricky salesman can sell anything.

definitions.definition: Relating to or associated with a prostitution trick
definitions.example: I don't want any of your tricky money, thank you!

definitions.definition: The thickest, or most active or intense, part of something.
definitions.example: It was mayhem in the thick of battle.

definitions.definition: A thicket.
A stupid person; a fool.
To thicken.
Relatively great in extent from one surface to the opposite in its smallest solid dimension.
Measuring a certain number of units in this dimension.
definitions.example: I want some planks that are two inches thick.

definitions.definition: Heavy in build; thickset.
definitions.example: He had such a thick neck that he had to turn his body to look to the side.

definitions.definition: Densely crowded or packed.
definitions.example: We walked through thick undergrowth.

definitions.definition: Having a viscous consistency.
definitions.example: My mum’s gravy was thick but at least it moved about.

definitions.definition: Abounding in number.
definitions.example: The room was thick with reporters.

definitions.definition: Impenetrable to sight.
definitions.example: We drove through thick fog.

definitions.definition: (Of an accent) Prominent, strong.
Stupid.
definitions.example: He was as thick as two short planks.

definitions.definition: Friendly or intimate.
definitions.example: They were as thick as thieves.

definitions.definition: Deep, intense, or profound.
definitions.example: Thick darkness.

definitions.definition: Troublesome; unreasonable
(chiefly of women) Curvy and voluptuous, and especially having large hips.
In a thick manner.
definitions.example: Snow lay thick on the ground.

definitions.definition: Frequently or numerously.
definitions.example: The arrows flew thick and fast around us.

definitions.definition: To touch repeatedly or stroke delicately in a manner which causes laughter, pleasure and twitching.
definitions.example: He tickled Nancy's tummy, and she started to giggle.

definitions.definition: To unexpectedly touch or stroke delicately in a manner which causes displeasure or withdrawal.
definitions.example: A stranger tickled Nancy's tummy, causing her to scream in fear.

definitions.definition: (of a body part) To feel as if the body part in question is being tickled.
definitions.example: My nose tickles, and I'm going to sneeze!

definitions.definition: To appeal to someone's taste, curiosity etc.
To cause delight or amusement in.
definitions.example: He was tickled to receive such a wonderful gift.

definitions.definition: To feel titillation.
To catch fish in the hand (usually in rivers or smaller streams) by manually stimulating the fins.
To be excited or heartened.
The ability to endure pain or hardship; endurance.
The ability or practice of tolerating; an acceptance of or patience with the beliefs, opinions or practices of others; a lack of bigotry.
The ability of the body (or other organism) to resist the action of a poison, to cope with a dangerous drug or to survive infection by an organism.
The variation or deviation from a standard, especially the maximum permitted variation in an engineering measurement.
definitions.example: Our customers can generally accept ten times the tolerance which we can achieve in our machining operations.

definitions.definition: The ability of the body to accept a tissue graft without rejection.
A small wheel or roller, specifically the wheel of a gun carriage.
The ball on top of a flagpole.

On a wooden mast, a circular disc (or sometimes a rectangle) of wood near or at the top of the mast, usually with holes or sheaves to reeve signal halyards; also a temporary or emergency place for a lookout. "Main" refers to the mainmast, whereas a truck on another mast may be called (on the mizzenmast, for example) "mizzen-truck".
A semi-tractor ("semi") trailer; a lorry.
definitions.example: Mexican open-bed trucks haul most of the fresh produce that comes into the United States from Mexico.

definitions.definition: Any motor vehicle designed for carrying cargo, including delivery vans, pickups, and other motorized vehicles (including passenger autos) fitted with a bed designed to carry goods.
A garden cart, a two-wheeled wheelbarrow.
A small wagon or cart, of various designs, pushed or pulled by hand or pulled by an animal, as with those in hotels for moving luggage, or in libraries for transporting books.
A pantechnicon (removal van).
A flatbed railway car; a flatcar.

A pivoting frame, one attached to the bottom of the bed of a railway car at each end, that rests on the axle and which swivels to allow the axle (at each end of which is a solid wheel) to turn with curves in the track; a bogie.
The part of a skateboard or roller skate that joins the wheels to the deck, consisting of a hanger, baseplate, kingpin, and bushings, and sometimes mounted with a riser in between.
A platform with wheels or casters.
Dirt or other messiness.
To drive a truck: Generally a truck driver's slang.
To convey by truck.
definitions.example: Last week, Cletus trucked 100 pounds of lumber up to Dubuque.

definitions.definition: To travel or live contentedly.
definitions.example: Keep on trucking!

definitions.definition: To persist, to endure.
definitions.example: Keep on trucking!

definitions.definition: (film production) To move a camera parallel to the movement of the subject.
To fight or otherwise physically engage with.
To run over or through a tackler in American football.
The quality of being decent; propriety.
That which is proper or becoming.
The act of conveying something.
definitions.example: The delivery was completed by four.

definitions.definition: The item which has been conveyed.
definitions.example: Your delivery is on the table.

definitions.definition: The act of giving birth
definitions.example: The delivery was painful.

definitions.definition: A pitching motion.
definitions.example: His delivery has a catch in it.

definitions.definition: A thrown pitch.
definitions.example: Here is the delivery; ... strike three!

definitions.definition: The manner of speaking.
definitions.example: The actor's delivery was flawless.

definitions.definition: The administration of a drug.
definitions.example: Drug delivery system.

definitions.definition: A ball bowled.
The process of throwing a stone.
Process of introducing foreign DNA into host cells.
A classification into two subclasses or opposed parts.
(projective geometry) The interchangeability of points and planes.
The mathematical equivalence of two seemingly different theoretical descriptions of a physical system.
To hang loosely with the ability to swing.
definitions.example: His feet would dangle in the water.

definitions.definition: The action of performing a move or deke with the puck in order to get past a defender or goalie; perhaps because of the resemblance to dangling the puck on a string.
definitions.example: He dangled around three players and the goalie to score.

definitions.definition: To hang or trail something loosely.
definitions.example: I like to sit on the edge and dangle my feet in the water.

definitions.definition: To trail or follow around.
Of a patient: to be positioned with the legs hanging over the edge of the bed.
To position (a patient) in this way.
The act of hanging something so that it can move freely; suspension.
The act of following or trailing around.
All out, open throttle, with all you have.
Suspended from above.
To damage beyond use or repair.
definitions.example: Hooligans destroy unprovoked.

definitions.definition: To neutralize, undo a property or condition.
definitions.example: Smoking destroys the natural subtlety of the palate.

definitions.definition: To put down or euthanize.
definitions.example: Destroying a rabid dog is required by law.

definitions.definition: To severely disrupt the well-being of (a person); ruin.
definitions.example: Her divorce destroyed her; she had a nervous breakdown and was severely depressed for more than a year.

definitions.definition: To defeat soundly.
To remove data.
definitions.example: The memory leak happened because we forgot to destroy the temporary lists.

definitions.definition: To sing a song poorly.
(antiphrasis) To exhaust duly and thus recreate or build up.
definitions.example: This exercise is going to destroy all parts of your shoulders.

definitions.definition: To penetrate sexually in an aggressive way.
definitions.example: She got destroyed by her lover on the carpet.

definitions.definition: A cobblestone.
A particle from 64 to 256 mm in diameter, following the Wentworth scale.
To make shoes (what a cobbler does).
To assemble in an improvised way.
definitions.example: I cobbled something together to get us through till morning.

definitions.definition: To use cobblestones to pave a road, walkway, etc.
Small flat-bottomed fishing boat suitable for launching from a beach, found on the north-east coast of England and in Scotland.
A room or set of rooms, particularly:
A chamberpot.
The legislature or division of the legislature itself.
definitions.example: The resolution, which speedily passed the Senate, was unable to gain a majority in the lower chamber.

definitions.definition: Any enclosed space occupying or similar to a room.
definitions.example: A canal lock chamber; a furnace chamber; a test chamber

definitions.definition: An enlarged space in an underground tunnel of a burrowing animal.
The area holding the ammunition round at the initiation of its discharge.
definitions.example: Dianne loaded a cartridge into the chamber of the rifle, then prepared to take aim at the target.

definitions.definition: One of the bullet-holding compartments in the cylinder of a revolver.
A short piece of ordnance or cannon which stood on its breech without any carriage, formerly used chiefly for celebrations and theatrical cannonades.
One of the two atria or two ventricles of the heart.
To enclose in a room.
definitions.example: She had chambered herself in her room, and wouldn't come out.

definitions.definition: To reside in or occupy a chamber or chambers.
To place in a chamber, as a round of ammunition.
definitions.example: The hunter fired at the geese and missed, then shrugged his shoulders and chambered another cartridge.

definitions.definition: To create or modify a gun to be a specific caliber.
definitions.example: The rifle was originally chambered for 9mm, but had since been modified for a larger, wildcat caliber.

definitions.definition: In martial arts, to prepare an offensive, defensive, or counteroffensive action by drawing a limb or weapon to a position where it may be charged with kinetic energy.
definitions.example: Bob chambered his fist for a blow, but Sheila struck first.

definitions.definition: To be lascivious.
A container used for urination and defecation, particularly those used in early modern towns before the advent of the flush toilet.
An alkaloid, C8H10N4O2, found naturally in tea and coffee plants which acts as a mild stimulant on the central nervous system.
A beverage made by infusing the beans of the coffee plant in hot water.
A serving of this beverage.
The seeds of the plant used to make coffee, misnamed ‘beans’ due to their shape.
The powder made by roasting and grinding the seeds.
A tropical plant of the genus Coffea.
A pale brown colour, like that of milk coffee.
The end of a meal, when coffee is served.
definitions.example: He did not stay for coffee.

definitions.definition: To drink coffee.
Of a pale brown colour, like that of milk coffee.
Of a table: a small, low table suitable for people in lounge seating to put coffee cups on.
(chiefly in the plural) documentary or electronic evidence that a person has certain status or privileges
definitions.example: May I see your credentials, please?

definitions.definition: To furnish with credentials
Documentary evidence of someone's right to credit or authority, especially such a document given to an ambassador by a country.
Official residence of a head of state or other dignitary, especially in a monarchical or imperial governmental system.
A large and lavishly ornate residence.
A large, ornate public building used for entertainment or exhibitions.
To decorate or ornate.
One who works in or with lead.
One who furnishes, fits, and repairs pipes and other apparatus for the conveyance of water, gas, or drainage.
A person who investigates or prevents leaks of information
In the Royal Navy, an apprentice, a boy aged 16 to 18, who is trained in technical skills at the Dockyard Schools to become an artificer.
A urologist.

In such writing systems as the Chinese writing system, the portion of a phono-semantic character that provides an indication of its pronunciation; contrasted with semantic (which is usually the radical).
Relating to the sounds of spoken language.
Relating to phones (as opposed to phonemes)
A small enclosure or field of grassland, especially for horses.
A field of grassland of any size, especially for keeping sheep or cattle.
An area where horses are paraded and mounted before a race and unsaddled after a race.
Land, fenced or otherwise delimited, which is most often part of a sheep or cattle property.
An area at circuit where the racing vehicles are parked and worked on before and between races.
(field sports) The playing field.
To provide with a paddock.
To keep in, or place in, a paddock.
A prediction, especially one made by a prophet or under divine inspiration.
definitions.example: French writer Nostradamus made a prophecy in his book.

definitions.definition: The public interpretation of Scripture.
To speak or write with divine inspiration; to act as prophet.
To predict, to foretell (with or without divine inspiration).
To foreshow; to herald; to prefigure.
To speak out on the Bible as an expression of holy inspiration; to preach.
The grand end of something, especially a show or piece of music.
The chronological conclusion of a series of narrative works.
A light, brief snowfall.
A sudden and brief blast or gust; a light, temporary breeze.
definitions.example: a flurry of wind

definitions.definition: A shower of dust, leaves etc. brought on by a sudden gust of wind.
Any sudden activity; a stir.
definitions.example: The day before the wedding was a flurry of preparations.

definitions.definition: A snack consisting of soft ice cream mixed with small pieces of fruit, cookie crumbs, etc.
The violent spasms of a dying whale.
An occurrence of something (countable instances) in large numbers, happening suddenly or in a short period of time.
definitions.example: The fencer landed a flurry of hits on her opponent.

definitions.definition: To agitate, bewilder, fluster.
To move or fall in a flurry.
Something which is not genuine, or is presented fraudulently.
A trick; a swindle.
A move meant to deceive an opposing player, used for gaining advantage for example when dribbling an opponent.
One of the circles or windings of a cable or hawser, as it lies in a coil; a single turn or coil.
A loose filmy mass or a thin chiplike layer of anything
definitions.example: There were a few flakes of paint on the floor from when we were painting the walls.

definitions.definition: A scale of a fish or similar animal
A prehistoric tool chipped out of stone.
A person who is impractical, flighty, unreliable, or inconsistent; especially with maintaining a living.
definitions.example: She makes pleasant conversation, but she's kind of a flake when it comes time for action.

definitions.definition: A carnation with only two colours in the flower, the petals having large stripes.
A flat turn or tier of rope.
To break or chip off in a flake.
definitions.example: The paint flaked off after only a year.

definitions.definition: To prove unreliable or impractical; to abandon or desert, to fail to follow through.
definitions.example: He said he'd come and help, but he flaked.

definitions.definition: To store an item such as rope or sail in layers
definitions.example: The line is flaked into the container for easy attachment and deployment.

definitions.definition: To hit (another person).
A paling; a hurdle.
A platform of hurdles, or small sticks made fast or interwoven, supported by stanchions, for drying codfish and other things.
A small stage hung over a vessel's side, for workmen to stand on while calking, etc.
A fortified defensive structure stationed with troops.
Any permanent army post.
An outlying trading-station, as in British North America.
To create a fort, fortifications, a strong point, or a redoubt.
A fictitious narrative intended to enforce some useful truth or precept, usually with animals, etc. as characters; an apologue. Prototypically, Aesop's Fables.
Any story told to excite wonder; common talk; the theme of talk.
Fiction; untruth; falsehood.
The plot, story, or connected series of events forming the subject of an epic or dramatic poem.
To compose fables; hence, to write or speak fiction; to write or utter what is not true.
To make up; to devise, and speak of, as true or real; to tell of falsely; to recount in the form of a fable.
The hair that grows over the bone ridge above the eye socket.
A dormer, usually of small size, whose roof line over the upright face is typically an arched curve, turning into a reverse curve to meet the horizontal line at either end.
A clump of waste fibres that builds up in a roller machine.
To build up waste fibres in a roller machine.
To signal with one's eyebrows.
A pair of small loudspeakers worn inside each outer ear or covering all or part of the ear, without a connecting band worn over head.
The action of entering, or going in.
definitions.example: Her entrance attracted no attention whatsoever.

definitions.definition: The act of taking possession, as of property, or of office.
definitions.example: the entrance of an heir upon his inheritance, or of a magistrate into office

definitions.definition: The place of entering, as a gate or doorway.
definitions.example: Place your bag by the entrance so that you can find it easily.

definitions.definition: The right to go in.
definitions.example: You'll need a ticket to gain entrance to the museum.

definitions.definition: The entering upon; the beginning, or that with which the beginning is made; the commencement; initiation.
definitions.example: a difficult entrance into business

definitions.definition: The causing to be entered upon a register, as a ship or goods, at a customhouse; an entering.
definitions.example: His entrance of the arrival was made the same day.

definitions.definition: The angle which the bow of a vessel makes with the water at the water line.
The bow, or entire wedgelike forepart of a vessel, below the water line.
When a musician starts playing or singing, entry.
The act of enrolling or the state of being enrolled.
The people enrolled, considered as a group.
The number of people enrolled.
The record of such enrolling; registration.
To die.
definitions.example: The patient expired in hospital.

definitions.definition: To lapse and become invalid.
definitions.example: My library card will expire next week.

definitions.definition: To exhale; to breathe out.
To give forth insensibly or gently, as a fluid or vapour; to emit in minute particles.
To bring to a close; to terminate.
That is no longer valid
That has been breathed out
Dead
One of a set of choices that can be made.
The freedom or right to choose.

A contract giving the holder the right to buy or sell an asset at a set strike price; can apply to financial market transactions, or to ordinary transactions for tangible assets such as a residence or automobile.
To purchase an option on something.
definitions.example: The new novel was optioned by the film studio, but they'll probably never decide to make a movie from it.

definitions.definition: To configure, by setting an option.
Something that overlaps or is overlapped

A situation in the game where an attacking line has more players in it than the defensive line coming to meet it. The attacking side may exploit the overlap by using their superior numbers to break the opposition's defensive line. If attackers outnumber defenders by more than one player this is often termed a two man overlap or three man overlap, etc. If the attacking side fails to break through usually due to poor execution, they are said to waste an overlap.
The payment of a spouse's or other dependant's annuity benefits concurrently with the member's benefits, on death of the member during the guarantee period.
To extend over and partly cover something.
definitions.example: The mouse-mat overlapped the edge of the desk.

definitions.definition: To have an area, range, character or function in common.
definitions.example: The two maps overlapped at the east and west edges.

definitions.definition: Of sets: to have some elements in common.
definitions.example: The set (all men) overlaps the set (vegetarians).

definitions.definition: To have some similar nucleotide sequences.
A property that is adjacent to the ocean.
The side of a property that faces the ocean.
Of property or real estate: adjacent to the ocean.
An excessive load.
The damage done, or the outage caused by such a load.
An overloaded version of a function.
To load excessively
To provide too much power to a circuit
To create different functions for the same name, to be used in different contexts
To fail due to excessive load
To arrange in working order.
To constitute in parts, each having a special function, act, office, or relation; to systematize.
(chiefly used in the past participle) To furnish with organs; to give an organic structure to; to endow with capacity for the functions of life
definitions.example: an organized being

definitions.definition: To sing in parts.
definitions.example: to organize an anthem

definitions.definition: To band together into a group or union that can bargain and act collectively; to unionize.
definitions.example: the workers decided to organize; their next task was to organize the workers at the steel mill

definitions.definition: Of a person, characterised by efficient organization.
definitions.example: Your work desk is so neat and tidy - I've never met someone so organized before!

definitions.definition: A mixture of unrelated things.
Items for a rummage sale.
A rummage sale.
To mix or confuse.
To meet or unite in a confused way.
definitions.example: I tried to study, but in my half-awake state, all of the concepts seemed to jumble together.

definitions.definition: A one-piece item of clothing originally used by parachutists
A similar item of clothing used for outdoor sports such as skiing
To shake something gently; to rattle or wiggle.
definitions.example: Jiggle the handle and see if the water stops.

definitions.definition: To shake, rattle, or wiggle.
definitions.example: The jelly jiggled in the bowl for a few moments after it was set down.

definitions.definition: A morbid condition, characterized by yellowness of the eyes, skin, and urine.
A feeling of bitterness, resentment or jealousy.
To affect with jaundice; to color by prejudice or envy; to prejudice.
A group of individuals chosen from the general population to hear and decide a case in a court of law.
A group of judges in a competition.
The audience attending the first night of a performance, whose reaction may determine whether it succeeds or fails.
To judge by means of a jury.
The act of supplying.
definitions.example: supply and demand

definitions.definition: An amount of something supplied.
definitions.example: A supply of good drinking water is essential.

definitions.definition: (in the plural) provisions.
(chiefly in the plural) An amount of money provided, as by Parliament or Congress, to meet the annual national expenditures.
definitions.example: to vote supplies

definitions.definition: Somebody, such as a teacher or clergyman, who temporarily fills the place of another; a substitute.
To provide (something), to make (something) available for use.
definitions.example: to supply money for the war

definitions.definition: To furnish or equip with.
definitions.example: to supply a furnace with fuel; to supply soldiers with ammunition

definitions.definition: To fill up, or keep full.
definitions.example: Rivers are supplied by smaller streams.

definitions.definition: To compensate for, or make up a deficiency of.
To serve instead of; to take the place of.
To act as a substitute.
To fill temporarily; to serve as substitute for another in, as a vacant place or office; to occupy; to have possession of.
definitions.example: to supply a pulpit

definitions.definition: A character or glyph representing an idea, concept or object.
definitions.example: $ is the symbol for dollars in the US and some other countries.

definitions.definition: A thing considered the embodiment of a concept or object.
definitions.example: The lion is the symbol of courage; the lamb is the symbol of meekness or patience.

definitions.definition: A type of noun whereby the form refers to the same entity independently of the context; a symbol arbitrarily denotes a referent. See also icon and index.
A summary of a dogmatic statement of faith.
definitions.example: The Apostles, Nicene Creed and the confessional books of Protestantism, such as the Augsburg Confession of Lutheranism are considered symbols.

definitions.definition: The numerical expression which defines a plane's position relative to the assumed axes.
That which is thrown into a common fund; hence, an appointed or accustomed duty.
Share; allotment.
An internal identifier used by a debugger to relate parts of the compiled program to the corresponding names in the source code.
A signalling event on a communications channel; a signal that cannot be further divided into meaningful information.
To symbolize.
The plant Crocus sativus, a crocus.
A spice (seasoning) and colouring agent made from the stigma and part of the style of the plant, sometimes or formerly also used as a dye and insect repellent.
An orange-yellow colour, the colour of a lion's pelt.
To add saffron to (a food), for taste, colour etc.
definitions.example: saffroned water, saffroned rice.

definitions.definition: To give a saffron colour to (something).
To embellish.
Having an orange-yellow colour.
definitions.example: The saffron robe of a Buddhist monk.

definitions.definition: A person's name, written by that person, used as identification or to signify approval of accompanying material, such as a legal contract.
An act of signing one's name; an act of producing a signature.
The part of a doctor’s prescription containing directions for the patient.
Signs on the stave indicating key and tempo, composed of the key signature and the time signature.
A group of four (or a multiple of four) sheets printed such that, when folded, they become a section of a book.
A pattern used for matching the identity of a virus, the parameter types of a method, etc.
Data attached to a message that guarantees that the message originated from its claimed source.
A mark or sign of implication.
A dish that is characteristic of a particular chef.
A tuple specifying the sign of coefficients in any diagonal form of a quadratic form.

A resemblance between the external character of a disease and those of some physical agent, for instance, that existing between the red skin of scarlet fever and a red cloth; supposed to indicate this agent in the treatment of the disease.

Text (or images, etc.) appended to a user's emails, newsgroup posts, forum posts, etc. as a way of adding a personal touch. Email signatures often include extended contact information. Forum signatures often serve as a way for a user to express themselves (song lyrics, art, etc.).
definitions.example: Your signature must not exceed 600 pixels.

definitions.definition: Distinctive, characteristic, indicative of identity.
definitions.example: Rabbit in mustard sauce is my signature dish.

definitions.definition: A seal which can magically transform into a human by shedding its skin.
A chicken of a certain breed with very fine, silk-like feathers.
Similar in appearance or texture (especially in softness and smoothness) to silk.
definitions.example: cloth with a silky lustre

definitions.definition: Smooth and pleasant; seductive.
definitions.example: a silky voice

definitions.definition: Covered in long, slender, glistening hairs pressed close to the surface; sericeous.
The study of all life or living matter.
The living organisms of a particular region.
The structure, function, and behavior of an organism or type of organism.
definitions.example: the biology of the whale

definitions.definition: The colour of the clear sky or the deep sea, between green and violet in the visible spectrum, and one of the primary additive colours for transmitted light; the colour obtained by subtracting red and green from white light using magenta and cyan filters; or any colour resembling this.
A blue dye or pigment.
Any of several processes to protect metal against rust.
Blue clothing
definitions.example: The boys in blue marched to the pipers.

definitions.definition: (in the plural) A blue uniform. See blues.
A member of law enforcement
The sky, literally or figuratively.
definitions.example: His request for leave came out of the blue.

definitions.definition: The ocean; deep waters.
The far distance; a remote or distant place.
Anything blue, especially to distinguish it from similar objects differing only in color.
A dog or cat with a slaty gray coat.
One of the colour balls used in snooker, with a value of five points.
Any of the butterflies of the subfamily Polyommatinae in the family Lycaenidae, most of which have blue on their wings.
A bluefish.
An argument.
A liquid with an intense blue colour, added to a laundry wash to prevent yellowing of white clothes.
A type of firecracker.
A bluestocking.
One of the three color charges for quarks.
To make or become blue.
To treat the surface of steel so that it is passivated chemically and becomes more resistant to rust.
(laundry) To brighten by treating with blue (laundry aid)
To spend (money) extravagantly; to blow.
Of the colour blue.
definitions.example: the deep blue sea

definitions.definition: Depressed, melancholic, sad.
Pale, without redness or glare; said of a flame.
definitions.example: The candle burns blue.

definitions.definition: Supportive of, run by (a member of), pertaining to, or dominated by a political party represented by the colour blue.
Of the higher-frequency region of the part of the electromagnetic spectrum which is relevant in the specific observation.
(of steak) Extra rare; left very raw and cold.
(of a dog or cat) Having a coat of fur of a slaty gray shade.
Severe or overly strict in morals; gloomy.
definitions.example: blue and sour religionists;  blue laws

definitions.definition: (of women) literary; bluestockinged.
Having a color charge of blue.
(entertainment) Risque or obscene
definitions.example: His material is too blue for prime-time

definitions.definition: Human conduct relative to social norms.
The way a living creature behaves or acts generally.
A state of probation about one's conduct.
definitions.example: He was on his best behavior when her family visited.

definitions.definition: An instance of the way a living creature behaves.
Observable response produced by an organism.
The way a device or system operates.
A signal fire to notify of the approach of an enemy, or to give any notice, commonly of warning.
A signal or conspicuous mark erected on an eminence near the shore, or moored in shoal water, as a guide to mariners.
A high hill or other easily distinguishable object near the shore which can serve as guidance for seafarers.
That which gives notice of danger, or keeps people on the correct path.
An electronic device that broadcasts a signal to nearby portable devices, enabling smartphones etc. to perform actions when in physical proximity to the beacon.
To act as a beacon.
To give light to, as a beacon; to light up; to illumine.
To furnish with a beacon or beacons.
A 12- to 13-year-old participant in the Young Women organization of the LDS Church.
An enclosed structure in which some species of honey bees (genus Apis) live and raise their young.
A man-made structure in which bees are kept for their honey.
Any place full of activity, or in which people are very busy.
A women's hairstyle, popular in the 1960s, in which long hair is styled into a hive-shaped form on top of the head and usually held in place with lacquer.
A particular style of hat.
A type of anti-personnel ammunition round containing flechettes, and characterised by the buzzing sound made as they fly through the air.
In Conway's Game of Life, a particular still life configuration with a rounded appearance.
To fill (a place) with busy activity.
A rooflike cover, usually of canvas, extended over or before any place as a shelter from the sun, rain, or wind.
That part of the poop deck which is continued forward beyond the bulkhead of the cabin.
A dissection performed on a cadaver to find possible cause(s) of death.
An after-the-fact examination, especially of the causes of a failure.
(rare) An eyewitness observation, the presentation of an event as witnessed.
To perform an autopsy on.
To perform an after-the-fact analysis of, especially of a failure.
Badly, terribly.
definitions.example: She led after the swiming and cycling, but ran awfully and came in fourth.

definitions.definition: Very; exceedingly; extremely; excessively.
In a manner inspiring awe.
Reverently.
Fearfully.
To succeed in something, now especially in academic performance.
To carry out successfully; to accomplish.
To conclude, finish, especially successfully.
To obtain, or gain (a desired result, objective etc.), as the result of exertion; to succeed in gaining; to win.
To conclude, to turn out.
To obtain (a material thing).
definitions.example: Show all the spoils by valiant kings achieved.

definitions.definition: (of a goal or status) Having been reached, attained or accomplished.
Abatement.
(obsolete outside law) To put an end to; to cause to cease.
definitions.example: to abate a nuisance

definitions.definition: To become null and void.
definitions.example: The writ has abated.

definitions.definition: To nullify; make void.
definitions.example: to abate a writ

definitions.definition: To humble; to lower in status; to bring someone down physically or mentally.
To be humbled; to be brought down physically or mentally.
To curtail; to deprive.
definitions.example: Order restrictions and prohibitions to abate an emergency situation.

definitions.definition: To reduce in amount, size, or value.
definitions.example: Legacies are liable to be abated entirely or in proportion, upon a deficiency of assets.

definitions.definition: To decrease in size, value, or amount.
To moderate; to lessen in force, intensity, to subside.
To decrease in intensity or force; to subside.
To deduct or omit.
definitions.example: We will abate this price from the total.

definitions.definition: To bar or except.
To cut away or hammer down, in such a way as to leave a figure in relief, as a sculpture, or in metalwork.
To dull the edge or point of; to blunt.
To destroy, or level to the ground.
Someone who is present; a bystander, a witness.
A person who assists or helps someone else.
Sales assistant.
A software tool that provides assistance in some task, a wizard program.
Having a subordinate or auxiliary position.
definitions.example: an assistant surgeon

definitions.definition: Helping; lending aid or support; auxiliary.
Abundant in growth or detail.
An item of confectionery consisting of a piece of candy/sweet attached to a stick.
A book in which measurements from the ship's log are recorded, along with other salient details of the voyage.
(by extension) A book in which events are recorded; a journal, especially of travel.
A record of the ownership, and licensing of a motor car
A vantage point with a view of the surrounding area.
A session of watching for an approaching enemy, police, etc.
definitions.example: We kept a lookout all night, but nobody came.

definitions.definition: A person on watch for approaching enemy, police, danger, etc.
definitions.example: The raid failed when the lookout noticed the enemy group.

definitions.definition: A subject for observation; a prospect or view.
One's perspective, outlook; hence, one's responsibility. (used with a possessive pronoun or a noun in a possessive form).
definitions.example: Every man's interest is his own lookout.
